content:
# Final Review Planner Positions
**Producer:** agent:claude/claude-opus-4-6

## Summary

All four amendments identify real defects. Three (fr-3-5e1074c4, fr-3-949a31d8, fr-3-1b3074a6) address task_id being silently stripped in the fs journal dedupe/merge/repair paths—render() hardcodes None, merge() skips the field, and explicitly_repaired_completion_event() also omits it. The existing test suite passes None for task_id in all cases, so these bugs are untested. The fourth (fr-3-62268f91) identifies that the daemon loop captures a structured next-step hint but only logs it, discarding a signal that was explicitly computed for downstream use.

## Votes

- `fr-3-5e1074c4`: Accept
  Confirmed: line 2857 passes None where self.task_id.as_deref() should go. CompletionJournalDetails is deserialized from JSON via parse() so task_id can be populated, but render() always emits None, silently stripping the field on any merge or repair re-serialization. The existing round-trip test (line 4727) uses None for task_id, so this bug is invisible to tests. Clear data-loss defect.
- `fr-3-949a31d8`: Accept
  Confirmed: merge() at lines 2836-2845 copies run_id, plan_hash, and outcome_detail from requested to merged when the merged value is None, but omits task_id entirely. It also conflict-checks run_id, plan_hash, and outcome_detail but not task_id. The test journal_merge_fills_all_optional_fields (line 4806) passes None for task_id in both cases, so it cannot catch this gap. When an existing event lacks task_id and the requested event provides one, the merge silently drops it.
- `fr-3-1b3074a6`: Accept
  This amendment covers the same ground as the previous two plus the explicitly_repaired_completion_event() path (lines 2982-2993), which also copies run_id, plan_hash, and outcome_detail but not task_id. While it overlaps with fr-3-5e1074c4 and fr-3-949a31d8, it correctly identifies three distinct code locations (merge, render, and explicit repair) that all fail to handle task_id, and its framing as a model-vs-adapter inconsistency is accurate—model.rs defines task_id (line 492-494) and render_completion_journal_details accepts it (line 504), but the fs adapter ignores it throughout.
- `fr-3-62268f91`: Accept
  Confirmed: try_reconcile_success() (daemon_loop.rs:1480) returns () and only println!s the hint at lines 1622-1627. The reconcile_success function explicitly captures a structured NextBeadResponse (success_reconciliation.rs:160-172) and returns it in ReconciliationOutcome, but the daemon consumer discards it. In a daemon context, stdout is not durable state—the structured signal computed by bv --robot-next is effectively lost. This is a real deficiency: the code invests in capturing and structuring a signal, then throws it away at the integration boundary.
